issues.title: You should not need to provide an openai api key in application.properties if you set it manually via code every time you call it
issues.body:
Please do a quick search on GitHub issues first, the feature you are about to request might have already been requested.

**Expected Behavior**

I do not set the spring.ai.openai.api-key in my application.properties, if i call a chatmodel that contains a key i get no error, if i call a chatmodel that does not contain an api key a get an error

<!--- Tell us how it should work. Add a code example to explain what you think the feature should look like. This is optional, but it would help up understand your expectations. -->

**Current Behavior**

if i run my application without setting an spring.ai.openai.api-keyi get the error 
Error creating bean with name 'openAiChatModel' defined in class path resource [org/springframework/ai/model/openai/autoconfigure/OpenAiChatAutoConfiguration.class]: Failed to instantiate [org.springframework.ai.openai.OpenAiChatModel]: Factory method 'openAiChatModel' threw exception with message: OpenAI API key must be set. Use the connection property: spring.ai.openai.api-key or spring.ai.openai.chat.api-key property.



<!--- Explain the difference from current behavior and why do you need this feature (aka why it is not possible to implement the desired functionality with the current version) -->

**Context**

My application is distributed to clients who provide their own key in a separate configuration file that is read at runtime, without this feature i need to include a dummy key to make the application run 
Another satisfactory solution would be to create an application.property setting to start the application without the key

<!--- 
How has this issue affected you?
What are you trying to accomplish?
What other alternatives have you considered?
Are you aware of any workarounds?
-->

comments.body:
You can set the value to _ and the application should start. 

```spring.ai.openai.api-key=_```

This is not a bug, neither a feature request.
